# `tf_program`

`tf_program` joins Python statements into a TensorFlow Python program.

```hcl
data "tensorflow_program" "main" {
  statements = [
    "x = tf.constant([1, 2, 3])",
    "print(tf.reduce_sum(x))",
  ]
}

output "python" {
  value = data.tensorflow_program.main.content
}
```

Generated Python:

```python
# Code generated by TF.tf. DO NOT EDIT.

import tensorflow as tf

x = tf.constant([1, 2, 3])
print(tf.reduce_sum(x))
```

## Custom Imports

Import values are written as Python import lines. Short specs are prefixed with
`import`.

```hcl
data "tensorflow_program" "main" {
  imports = [
    "tensorflow as tf",
    "from pathlib import Path",
  ]

  statements = [
    "print(Path.cwd())",
    "print(tf.__version__)",
  ]
}
```

## Custom Header

```hcl
data "tensorflow_program" "main" {
  header = "# Generated for an example."

  statements = [
    "print(tf.__version__)",
  ]
}
```
